enGB: This is a test feat, how very jank.

Acrobats are people who learned that the easiest way through life is rarely the most direct, and that flexibility and quick thinking are the most important things to stay alive in dire situations. While they generally prefer to rely on their skills and natural charisma to avoid confrontation when possible, acrobats are capable fighters. The acrobats signature fighting style tends to favor disrupting their opponents rather than simply overpowering them, which reflects their intricate lifestyles and distaste for directness.

Acrobats are masters of combat maneuvers and excel at applying different conditions on enemies. They are lightly armored but are able to easily position themselves to avoid being targeted and are agile enough to avoid some hits coming their way.

An acrobat is proficient with all simple weapons plus the flail, heavy flail, two-bladed sword and nuchaku. When using one of these extra weapons or a quarterstaff, the acrobat can treat them as light weapons for the purpose of using Weapon Finesse. They are proficient with light armor, but not with shields.

At 2nd level, and every 4th level afterwards, an acrobat gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. These bonus feats must be selected from those listed as Combat Maneuver Feats. The acrobat can select a bonus feat even if they do not meet its attribute or feat requirements.
At 1st level, an acrobat gets a +1 bonus to their CMB and CMD. That bonus increases by 1 at 5th level and every 4th level afterwards.
At 1st level and every 8th level afterward, an acrobat chooses a maneuver type to become their specialty. When performing a specialty maneuver, the acrobat's Maneuver Mastery bonus to CMB is doubled.

At 2nd level and every 3rd level afterwards, an acrobat gains one opportunistic manipulation.  An opportunistic manipulation can either be used as a standard action or be set as the acrobat's active manipulation. Whenever the acrobat misses a melee attack, they will perform their active manipulation against the attack's target. An acrobat can only perform one opportunistic manipulation per turn, further misses will have no effect.  Some opportunistic manipulations allow their target to make a saving throw, the DC of these saves is equal to 10 + half the acrobat's level + the acrobat's Charisma modifier.
